title: command line switch
text: command line switch An argument or qualifier added to the command line of a Uniface application executable (such as ide or uniface ), that instructs the application to perform a certain task or behave in a certain way for the duration of that application session. For example, the /frm command line switch instructs the Uniface Development Environment to compile forms in the repository. Related Links Command Line Switches Reference Command line switches can be used with Uniface executables to specify configuration files, run utilities, supply parameters, apply operating constraints, and otherwise qualify the session. They remain in effect for the duration of that application session. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
